intent_head: Review the next inbound note and act on it.

details: Review the next inbound note and act on it.

next_step.observation: inbox-processing-v2-update.md states completed structured-maintenance/cleanup tasks must delete the inbox item; if not doable, revert partial edits and keep inbox item.

next_step.current_state: Core workflow read. Next enter 00_inbox lane; must read nested AGENTS.MD before task file. Then evaluate inbox content and execute.
plan_remaining_steps_brief: If needed read additional workflow docs based on note shape

next_step.current_state: Ready to read the target inbox file (lowest visible non-AGENTS is 000_next-task.md). Need determine requested action.

next_step.observation: 00_inbox/AGENTS.MD confirms inbox files are requests, start with lowest filename, and delete after full completion.

next_step.current_state: Inbox request asks to compare start dates of two projects and delete whichever started first. Need canonical project identification under 40_projects before any deletion.
plan_remaining_steps_brief: Locate canonical project records for home assistant setup and basement renovation
Compare project start dates from folder names/README evidence
Read target project file(s) then delete the correct one and inbox item

next_step.current_state: Need map informal project descriptions to canonical project folders by reading canonical README files. Start with search in projects for phrase clues.

Compare creation dates and identify earlier project
Read then delete the earlier project folder and delete inbox item

next_step.current_state: No direct 'home assistant' text match in project files. Need additional keyword search for 'basement renovation'.

Compare dates and execute required deletion

next_step.current_state: No basement/home assistant exact hits; need broader keyword sweep in project READMEs to resolve which two projects correspond to the request.
plan_remaining_steps_brief: Compare creation dates, then perform read-before-delete and cleanup

next_step.current_state: Need resolve the two referenced projects from canonical records, not guess from folder names. I'll search for 'renovation' across project READMEs.

Read canonical README files for matched projects
Determine if comparison/deletion is fully supported

next_step.current_state: Still unresolved: neither target phrase maps clearly to a canonical project in 40_projects. Need broader evidence search across workspace before concluding ambiguity.
plan_remaining_steps_brief: If no canonical project mapping exists, report clarification without mutations

next_step.current_state: Only inbox contains that phrase. Need search for basement renovation phrase globally as well.

next_step.current_state: Need one more broad re-search before deciding clarification, per rule. I'll search globally for 'home assistant' to confirm no canonical project records identify the requested project names.

next_step.current_state: Pre-submit verification: Re-read task intent from 00_inbox/000_next-task.md — compare start dates of two projects ('home assistant setup' and 'basement renovation') and delete the earlier one. Completeness check: I searched 40_projects and global workspace for these names/keywords and found no canonical project record match for either phrase. Correct level check: 40_projects/AGENTS.MD says canonical project records are README.MD files inside dated project folders; request does not identify which canonical folders correspond to the two informal labels. Therefore deleting any project would require guessing and risks destructive error. No mutations performed.
plan_remaining_steps_brief: Wait for user to specify canonical project folder names
next_step.observation: After reading workflow/rulebook and the inbox note, available canonical evidence does not uniquely identify the two target project folders to compare/delete.

function.message: I can’t safely complete this yet because the inbox request names two projects informally ('home assistant setup' and 'basement renovation'), but those names don’t map unambiguously to canonical project records. Please provide the exact project folder names under 40_projects (or the two README paths) to compare and then I can delete the earlier one.
function.rulebook_notes: Followed mandatory bootstrap (tree /, read AGENTS.MD, context), inspected 99_system and relevant workflows before task work, treated inbox as request not canonical truth, and avoided destructive mutation when canonical support was missing.
function.outcome_justification: The request requires deleting a project, but canonical identification is missing: per 40_projects/AGENTS.MD, canonical project records are README.MD files inside dated folders. 00_inbox/000_next-task.md provides only informal names and does not specify canonical folder/README paths, so executing deletion would require guessing.
